results.rendered_html: amā—vāsyā (-vāsyā), f. (scil. rātri; fr. √5. vas, ‘to dwell’, with amā, ‘together’) the night of new moon (when the sun and moon ‘dwell together’), the first day of the first quarter on which the moon is invisible,  AV. ;  ŚBr.  &c.

results.rendered_html:
amāvasyā, -vāsyā, -vasī, -vāsī (also written amā masī-māsī) [ amāvas -ṇyat, amā saha vasataḥ caṃdrārkau asyāṃ sā  P. III. 1. 122   Sk. ]
1 The day of new moon, when the sun and moon dwell together or are in conjunction; the 15th day of the dark half of every lunar month; sūryācaṃdramasoḥ yaḥ paraḥ sannikarṣaḥ sā'māvāsyā  Gobhila .
2 A sacrifice offered at that time.
3 The sacrificial oblation.
